sourceMovie.title: Police Story 4: First Strike
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Police Story 4: First Strike for the same reason it broke through in 1996: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Stanley Tong Gwai-Lai keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: RED
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Police Story 4: First Strike clicked for you, RED (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Police Story 4: First Strike?
faq.answer: Try RED, The Jackal, The Losers—each shares DNA with Police Story 4: First Strike while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Police Story 4: First Strike?
faq.answer: Police Story 4: First Strike (1996) is best described as Action, Adventure, Comedy, Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Police Story 4: First Strike part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Police Story Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
